questions.text: What is the primary purpose of a BIP39 mnemonic phrase?

options.text: To encrypt private keys before storing them on a blockchain.
To serve as a human-readable backup that can deterministically regenerate all private keys in an HD wallet.
To act as a password for logging into a crypto exchange.
To prove ownership of a wallet to a blockchain validator.

questions.explanation: The mnemonic encodes entropy that, combined with an optional passphrase, can derive the master seed for an HD wallet. From this seed, all private keys and addresses can be regenerated.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

The mnemonic encodes entropy that, combined with an optional passphrase, can derive the master seed for an HD wallet. From this seed, all private keys and addresses can be regenerated.

questions.text: A 12-word BIP39 mnemonic encodes how many bits of entropy?

options.text: 64 bits
128 bits
256 bits
132 bits (entropy only, excluding checksum)

questions.explanation: 12 words × 11 bits/word = 132 bits total. The last 4 bits are the checksum, so the underlying entropy is 128 bits. 24-word mnemonics encode 256 bits of entropy.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

12 words × 11 bits/word = 132 bits total. The last 4 bits are the checksum, so the underlying entropy is 128 bits. 24-word mnemonics encode 256 bits of entropy.

questions.text: What is the standard BIP44 derivation path for the first external address on an Ethereum-compatible wallet (including MST)?
questions.explanation: BIP44 assigns coin type 60' to Ethereum. The path m/44'/60'/0'/0/0 represents purpose 44', coin 60', account 0', change 0 (external), address index 0. All EVM chains reuse this path for compatibility.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

BIP44 assigns coin type 60' to Ethereum. The path m/44'/60'/0'/0/0 represents purpose 44', coin 60', account 0', change 0 (external), address index 0. All EVM chains reuse this path for compatibility.

questions.text: Why does BIP44 use hardened derivation (apostrophe) for the account level?

options.text: To make the derivation path shorter.
To ensure that a compromised child private key cannot be used to derive sibling keys or the parent key.
To allow public key derivation for watch-only wallets.
To make the mnemonic easier to remember.

questions.explanation: Hardened derivation uses the parent private key (not public key) in the HMAC computation. This means that even if an attacker obtains a child private key and the chain code, they cannot derive parent or sibling keys. It isolates accounts from each other.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

Hardened derivation uses the parent private key (not public key) in the HMAC computation. This means that even if an attacker obtains a child private key and the chain code, they cannot derive parent or sibling keys. It isolates accounts from each other.

questions.text: Which of the following is the BEST practice for storing a mnemonic phrase for a wallet holding significant value?

options.text: Save it in a password manager like LastPass.
Stamp it onto a metal plate and store it in a secure, offline location (e.g., a safe deposit box).
Email it to yourself as an encrypted PDF.
Take a screenshot and store it in a private Google Photos album.

questions.explanation: Digital storage of any kind (password manager, email, cloud) exposes the mnemonic to malware, breaches, and remote attacks. A physical, durable medium (metal) stored offline is the gold standard.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct: B

Digital storage of any kind (password manager, email, cloud) exposes the mnemonic to malware, breaches, and remote attacks. A physical, durable medium (metal) stored offline is the gold standard.

questions.text: "If you use an additional passphrase (the '13th word') with your BIP39 mnemonic, losing the passphrase is equivalent to losing the mnemonic - the wallet cannot be recovered."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states TRUE.+1 mark: Justification explains that the passphrase changes the PBKDF2 seed output, that a different passphrase yields a completely different wallet, and that recovery without the exact passphrase is practically impossible.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states TRUE.
+1 mark: Justification explains that the passphrase changes the PBKDF2 seed output, that a different passphrase yields a completely different wallet, and that recovery without the exact passphrase is practically impossible.

questions.text: "Since MST is an EVM-compatible chain, a MetaMask wallet created with a BIP39 mnemonic can be imported into MST's BridgeKey wallet, and the same 0x address will control the same assets on both chains."
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Correctly states TRUE.+1 mark: Justification explains that EVM compatibility ensures identical key derivation and address generation, and that the same mnemonic produces the same 0x address across all EVM chains. Must note that assets are chain-specific.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

1 mark: Correctly states TRUE.
+1 mark: Justification explains that EVM compatibility ensures identical key derivation and address generation, and that the same mnemonic produces the same 0x address across all EVM chains. Must note that assets are chain-specific.
